architecture.summary: This paper investigates voice-based intelligent personal assistants (IPAs) for crewed space missions through human-centered design research. It does not describe a trained system but rather identifies design requirements and proposes architectural guidelines combining retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), knowledge graphs, and augmented reality to enable flexible, reliable, offline-capable, and multimodal IPAs for astronauts operating without real-time Earth communication.

biblio.title: Advancing Intelligent Personal Assistants for Human Spaceflight

claims.statement: Through semi-structured interviews with astronauts (n=3) and spaceflight experts (n=12), thematic analysis reveals core requirements for flexibility, reliability, offline capability, and multimodal interaction in intelligent personal assistants for space missions

Technologies such as retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), knowledge graphs, and augmented reality should be combined to support flexible, reliable, and multimodal IPAs for future human spaceflight missions
Voice-based intelligent personal assistants are needed to support astronaut autonomy in the absence of real-time communication with Earth during Artemis program and Mars missions
